landing.hero.headline: Build a Hooper — Spin, build, simulate your perfect 82-game season.
landing.hero.subheadline: Build a Hooper lets you draft team-inspired skills, shape your player strengths, and test roster logic across a full season — right in your browser at buildahooper.org.
landing.features.title: Why players keep coming back to Build a Hooper
landing.features.description: The loop is simple to start and surprisingly deep once you begin optimizing a full season. Every run is a small basketball experiment.
landing.features.auth.title: Authentication
landing.features.auth.description: Email/password, Google, and GitHub OAuth — ready out of the box with session management and account linking.
landing.features.payment.title: Payments
landing.features.payment.description: Stripe, PayPal, Alipay, WeChat Pay — checkout, subscriptions, webhooks, and order management.
landing.features.rbac.title: Roles & Permissions
landing.features.rbac.description: Fine-grained RBAC with wildcard matching. Full admin panel for managing users, roles, and permissions.
landing.features.i18n.title: Internationalization
landing.features.i18n.description: English, Chinese, Japanese, and Korean built-in with locale-aware routing. Easy to extend with additional languages.
landing.features.cms.title: Content Management
landing.features.cms.description: Categories and posts with full CRUD, status management, and admin panel — build your blog or docs.
landing.features.credits.title: Credit System
landing.features.credits.description: FIFO consumption, expiration, auto-grant on signup, and revocation — ready for usage-based billing.
landing.pricing.title: Pricing
landing.pricing.description: Choose the plan that works for you.
landing.faq.title: Frequently asked questions
landing.faq.description: Quick answers for players who want to understand the game before or while playing.
landing.faq.stack.question: What's the tech stack?
landing.faq.stack.answer: Next.js 16 with the App Router, React 19, TypeScript strict mode, Tailwind CSS 4, shadcn/ui, Drizzle ORM, and better-auth. Every piece of business logic is inlined — no external packages for payment, email, storage, or AI.
landing.faq.payment.question: Which payment providers are supported?
landing.faq.payment.answer: Stripe, PayPal, Creem, Alipay, and WeChat Pay out of the box. The PaymentManager abstraction makes it straightforward to add more providers by implementing a single interface.
landing.faq.database.question: Which databases can I use?
landing.faq.database.answer: Drizzle ORM handles PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, Turso, and Cloudflare D1. Switch providers by editing DATABASE_PROVIDER in your .env — the schema templates and drizzle config adapt automatically.
landing.faq.customize.question: How customizable is the landing page?
landing.faq.customize.answer: The src/blocks/ directory ships with demo sections — they're designed to be rewritten per project. The durable primitives live in src/components/, so your rebrand touches blocks and translations, not the chassis.
landing.faq.license.question: Can I use this for commercial projects?
landing.faq.license.answer: Yes. ShipAny Next is built for shipping production SaaS products. You own the code after cloning — no per-seat fees, no runtime dependencies on our infrastructure.
landing.cta.headline: Ready to ship your SaaS?
landing.cta.subheadline: Stop wiring auth, payments, and RBAC from scratch. Start with ShipAny Next and build what actually makes your product unique.
landing.footer.tagline: Draft skills. Build your player. Chase the season.

landing.meta.title: Build a Hooper - Play Online, Learn the Modes, Master the Season
landing.meta.description: Build a Hooper is an unofficial browser basketball simulator where you draft skills, tune attributes, explore game modes, and push through an 82-game season.
landing.hero.embed_fallback: If the game does not load in the embedded frame, open it in a new tab.
landing.features.draft.title: Draft skills with identity
landing.features.draft.description: Each run begins with choices tied to recognizable basketball archetypes. Your build has direction before the season even starts.
landing.features.build.title: Shape a player around strengths
landing.features.build.description: Attributes define how consistently your player performs, how strengths compound, and where a build can break under pressure.
landing.features.modes.title: Adjust to different modes
landing.features.modes.description: Three modes reward different priorities, so experimentation is part of the fun instead of an afterthought.
landing.features.season.title: Think beyond one hot streak
landing.features.season.description: An 82-game season rewards durable builds, not flashy peaks. Long-term balance and roster logic matter more than one lucky run.

landing.how.title: How Build a Hooper works
landing.how.description: Treat every run as a basketball theory test: can this build survive an entire season?
landing.how.step1.title: 1. Draft a direction
landing.how.step1.description: Start by choosing skills that point toward a clear identity instead of collecting disconnected bonuses.
landing.how.step2.title: 2. Build around attributes
landing.how.step2.description: Use the attribute spread to support the style you want to play. Efficiency comes from synergy more than isolated numbers.
landing.how.step3.title: 3. Survive the season
landing.how.step3.description: The real test is whether your choices still make sense after many games, when consistency separates good builds from fragile ones.
landing.guide.title: Build a Hooper guide, tips, and season strategy
landing.guide.intro: Build a Hooper turns a familiar sports fantasy into a fast, replayable browser simulation. You are not only clicking through games — you are building a theory about what kind of player wins over time. The public game page highlights 13 attributes, 3 modes, and an 82-game season structure, which already tells you this is less about one heroic moment and more about sustained decision-making. The fun starts with the draft, but the depth appears when you realize every skill choice changes how attributes should be valued and how much risk your season can tolerate. That is why the game feels better when you stop hunting for one perfect build and start thinking in terms of archetypes, trade-offs, and season-long consistency.
landing.guide.attributes_body: With 13 attributes in play, no single stat can carry everything. The strongest runs usually come from builds where several strengths reinforce each other. If your draft leans toward explosive offense, you still need enough balance to avoid collapsing during a long season. If your choices favor all-around stability, the goal becomes maximizing repeatable value rather than chasing the most dramatic peak. Beginners often overreact to the most exciting option on the board, but better results come from asking which attribute investments make the entire build more coherent. Think about finishing, creation, defense, physical tools, and decision-making as parts of one identity — not as isolated upgrades. When two or three attributes support the same role, your player becomes easier to read and easier to improve across multiple runs.
landing.guide.modes_title: How to approach the different modes
landing.guide.modes_body: Because Build a Hooper includes three modes, adaptation matters more than a universal recipe. Some modes may reward aggression and quick payoff, while others punish imbalance over time. Do not assume your first successful build transfers perfectly everywhere. When you switch modes, re-evaluate the same strengths through a new lens: does this setup depend on short bursts, does it need consistency, or does it rely on one weakness never being exposed? Treat each mode as a new environment for testing your basketball logic. A build that dominates in one context might look merely average in another — and that is useful information. The practical habit is to run the same archetype across modes with small adjustments, then compare where it holds up and where it breaks. That kind of structured experimentation teaches you more than rerolling blindly for luck.
landing.guide.season_body: The 82-game structure is central to the fantasy. A long season shifts the goal from momentary excitement to repeatable performance. Strong season builds usually have a plan for average nights, not just peak nights. They win because they keep producing when novelty wears off. Value stability, role clarity, and compounding strengths. A build that looks unstoppable early can fade if it depends on everything breaking perfectly. Ask whether your player still makes sense in game 40, game 60, and game 82. Can the build recover from a cold stretch? Does it have enough balance to survive matchups that expose its weakness? The best players in Build a Hooper are often not the most dramatic — they are the ones whose strengths still matter late in the schedule.
landing.guide.beginner_body: If you are new, commit to one identity per run. Try an offensive build, a balanced build, and a stability-first build. After each attempt, ask what failed first. Did the player lack support around a signature strength? Did the season expose a weak area that seemed harmless at the draft stage? Did a mode reward a pacing strategy you ignored? Write down one lesson before starting again. This reflection teaches more than chasing a lucky roll. Build a Hooper becomes much more satisfying when you read each season as feedback on your decisions rather than random noise.
landing.guide.search_title: Why players search for Build a Hooper guides
landing.guide.search_body: Search interest around Build a Hooper usually comes from the same questions: Is there a best build? Do attributes matter more than skills? How much does mode selection change strategy? The honest answer is that the game becomes more interesting when you abandon the idea of one solved formula. The better question is how to create a build that fits the mode, survives the season, and stays internally consistent. Once you approach the simulator that way, the draft becomes more meaningful, the season becomes easier to read, and your improvements become easier to repeat. That is exactly what this guide is for — helping you play smarter, not just play more.
landing.also_play.description: Spin five team-seasons, spend $100M on a starting five, and see if you can beat the 2015–16 Warriors.
landing.faq.what.question: What is Build a Hooper?
landing.faq.what.answer: Build a Hooper is an unofficial browser basketball simulator focused on drafting skills, shaping a custom player, and testing your build across a full 82-game season.
landing.faq.attributes.question: How do attributes affect performance?
landing.faq.attributes.answer: Attributes shape how reliably your build expresses its strengths. With 13 attributes advertised on the game page, synergy and balance usually matter more than one isolated number.
landing.faq.beginners.question: What should beginners focus on first?
landing.faq.beginners.answer: Start by choosing a clear identity for each run. Builds become easier to understand when your skill draft and attribute priorities support the same direction.
landing.faq.season.question: Why does season strategy matter so much?
landing.faq.season.answer: A full 82-game season rewards consistency. A flashy build can look great early and still fade if it lacks balance, durability, or adaptability.
landing.faq.official.question: Is this an official NBA game?
landing.faq.official.answer: No. Build a Hooper is presented here as an unofficial basketball simulator and is not affiliated with the NBA or official team branding.
landing.disclaimer.title: Unofficial game notice
landing.disclaimer.body: Build a Hooper is an unofficial basketball simulator experience. buildahooper.org is a fan-oriented portal that embeds the publicly available game and provides original explanatory content, strategy notes, and localized guidance. It is not affiliated with the NBA or with any official league, team, logo, photo, or uniform rights holder.
landing.footer.copyright: © 2026 Build a Hooper. All rights reserved.

game.guide.title: How to play Build a Hooper
game.guide.intro: Build a Hooper is a browser basketball builder: you draft skills from legendary and modern team-seasons, lock thirteen attributes into a custom player, join a career franchise, and simulate an 82-game season toward a championship. This page is the live game plus a practical primer so you know what each mode hides, what each attribute means, and how a first run should feel before you chase rings.
game.guide.modes_title: Modes: what you see while drafting
game.guide.modes_body: Modes do not change the thirteen attributes. They change information and rerolls. Pick the mode that matches how much uncertainty you want before the season grades your sheet.
game.guide.mode_classic: Classic — choose your position, see player grades, and keep three team rerolls. Best for learning how scoring, creation, and defense clusters stack.
game.guide.mode_blind: Blind — your position is revealed first, but grades stay hidden until the final reveal. You commit earlier with only one reroll.
game.guide.mode_chaos: Chaos — position and grades stay dark until the end. Builds that need perfect information fall apart here; plan a second skill path.
game.guide.attrs_body: Every build fills the same sheet: 3PT, MID, FIN, DNK, HAN, PAS, PDEF, IDEF, BLK, REB, ATH, STR, and CLU. Treat them as clusters, not thirteen unrelated upgrades. After three locks, name the role out loud — floor general, paint bully, two-way wing — or you are collecting grades instead of building a player.
game.guide.attrs_scoring: Scoring — 3PT, MID, FIN, DNK shape how you put the ball in the basket from deep, mid-range, the paint, and above the rim.
game.guide.attrs_creation: Creation — HAN and PAS decide whether you keep possessions alive and set teammates up under pressure.
game.guide.attrs_defense: Defense / glass — PDEF, IDEF, BLK, and REB cover perimeter stops, interior contests, shot-blocking, and board work.
game.guide.attrs_physical: Physical / close — ATH, STR, and CLU cover burst, body strength, and late-game execution when the season gets loud.
game.guide.tutorial_1: Start in Classic. Pick a position you understand (PG creators or SF wings are forgiving teachers).
game.guide.tutorial_2: Spin a team-season, pick one roster player, and lock one open attribute. Repeat until all thirteen slots are filled — one lock per round.
game.guide.tutorial_3: Use rerolls when the roster cannot support your identity. Do not spend them chasing every A+; protect the cluster that defines the card.
game.guide.tutorial_4: Reveal the hooper, spin a career team, then simulate the 82-game season. Watch awards, win totals, and whether the build still makes sense by game 60.
game.guide.tutorial_5: Sign in before finishing if you want the run saved for achievements and legacy tracking. Guests can learn the loop; progress sticks after login.
game.guide.tutorial_outro: When Classic feels readable, move to Blind, then Chaos. Mode badges and harder achievement ladders lean on those darker drafts — but your first job is one honest season, not a perfect sheet.
achievements.guide.title: How the achievement system works
achievements.guide.intro: Achievements turn each finished Build a Hooper season into a lasting stamp. The catalog has 52 goals across eight categories — modes, competitive titles, positions, rookies, underdogs, builds, awards, and legacy records. Points on this board are separate from Legacy rank on the leaderboard; both feed on saved runs after you sign in.
achievements.guide.unlock_body: Nothing pops mid-simulation. A season ends on My Card. When you are logged in, the run saves, and this page recomputes progress from your career history.
achievements.guide.unlock_1: Play a full run on /game and finish the season while signed in.
achievements.guide.unlock_2: Return here — badges unlock from the saved career, not from mid-season screenshots.
achievements.guide.unlock_3: Use filters for locked/unlocked, category, and rarity, or search by name to plan the next grind.
achievements.guide.unlock_note: Several ring ladders only count Chaos and Blind championships. Classic still fills mode, position, and many build badges, but Dynasty, Complete Hooper, underdogs, and Future GOATs lean on darker modes by design.
achievements.guide.categories_body: Modes cover first finishes and mode rings. Competitive titles climb Chaos/Blind ring counts (5 / 11 / 25 / 50). Positions award a title at each spot and Complete Hooper for all five in Chaos or Blind. Rookies and underdogs reward young or low-overall sheets. Builds read attribute shapes such as Sniper or Defensive Wall. Awards map Scoring Title, MVP, DPOY, and Finals MVP combos. Legacy covers triple-doubles, 60/70/73-win seasons, franchise first banners, play-in miracles, and Finals comebacks.
achievements.guide.table_intro: Each unlock grants achievement points by rarity. Use this chart to compare how season milestones pay on the board — from first-mode finishes to GOAT-tier records.
achievements.guide.table.common_note: First Classic / Blind / Chaos finishes and early onboarding stamps.
achievements.guide.table.rare_note: Single mode rings, starter position titles, and simple build shapes.
achievements.guide.table.epic_note: Multi-run ladders, tougher builds, and mid-tier award combos.
achievements.guide.table.legendary_note: Deep ring counts, Complete Hooper progress, and hard legacy marks.
achievements.guide.table.special_note: Rare season stories — underdog miracles, specialty franchise banners.
achievements.guide.table.goat_note: Ceiling records such as 73-9 Club and 3-1 Finals comebacks.
achievements.guide.table_outro: Full board totals 4200 points. Achievement points do not add into leaderboard Legacy — treat this page as the checklist career, and the leaderboard as the ranked career.
